checks.finding: No issues found. T-001 (tasks.md ~111-125): Risk: low, docs-only WFI-lifecycle-prose + trailing-column append, no control-flow/data/security impact -- matches risk-classification-policy.md low tier. T-002 (tasks.md ~273-292): Risk: medium; DATA-ONLY population of contracts/agent-model-capabilities.v2.json within Pillar C's already-defined schema, verification is external/mechanical (Pillar C's parity suite + agent-model-routing.tests.sh re-run green, AC-014); no sentinel surface (auth/authz/payment/PII/migration/external-API) is touched -- consistent with medium. T-003 (tasks.md ~445-466): Risk: high; creates a new scheduled GitHub Actions workflow holding issues:write processing untrusted external content that can reach a publicly-posted issue body (AC-021), plus this spec's one protected enforcement-chain-file touch (test.yml via human-copy, AC-011) -- matches the high-tier 'sensitive surface... or anything where a silent defect causes material harm' bucket; Required Workflow: tdd correctly derived. No over- or under-classification found.

T-003 is the spec's only Risk: high/critical task. Its Done When section (tasks.md ~656-663) states: 'TDD evidence is recorded in the implementation report with Red and Green explicitly separated: RED -- the authored suite run against the pre-landing tree ... failing meaningfully; GREEN -- the post-commit-A run of TEST-005..007/009/010/016/020/021 passing ..., re-confirmed after commit B,' satisfying Red->Green evidence, and closes with 'An independent quality-gate verdict records PASS for this task,' matching this repo's independent-review convention (tasks.md Lifecycle: 'Only quality-gate may set Done', a role distinct from implement-task). No critical-tier task exists, so second-approver/signed-bundle requirements do not apply.

T-003 ('Add the weekly model-freshness-check automation', tasks.md ~437-687) is oversized on two independent indicators. (1) Its Done When list (tasks.md ~613-663) contains 13 distinct verifiable items (TEST-005, TEST-006, TEST-007, TEST-008, TEST-009, TEST-010, TEST-011, TEST-016, TEST-020, TEST-021, TEST-018's #157 leg, TEST-019's #157 leg, and the TDD-evidence bullet), exceeding the 'more than eight distinct verifiable items' threshold by 5. (2) Commit A's Scope spans at least six distinct implementation areas bundled into one task: the TDD-Red-first fixture-driven test-suite twin (.sh AND .ps1, design.md API/Contract Plan steps 1-9), the three-function check-model-freshness.sh script, the model-freshness-check.yml workflow, tests/run-all.sh/.ps1 registration, human-copy staging of the protected test.yml candidate + MANIFEST.sha256, and AC-008's one-time manual workflow_dispatch verification against a disposable fixture branch. Failure mode: a task this large is unlikely to be completable in a single focused implementation session, raising the risk of a stalled Red state, partial landing, or a skipped Done-When item under time pressure. Recommend splitting (e.g., extracting human-copy staging + AC-008 manual verification into a follow-on task, or separating workflow+script authoring from test-suite-twin authoring).

T-002 (functional, medium-risk): AC-013/TEST-013 is a negative/hygiene assertion (v1 registry byte-for-byte unchanged, hash comparison) and AC-014/TEST-014 requires Pillar C's parity suite plus tests/agent-model-routing.tests.sh to stay green after the edit, serving as regression/error-path protection against a malformed data edit. T-003 (functional, high-risk) has extensive edge-case coverage: AC-006/TEST-006 (three fetch-failure scenarios, fail-soft), AC-007/TEST-007 (dedup negative branch), AC-020/TEST-020 (no-diff/zero-side-effect branch), AC-021/TEST-021 (adversarial-payload allowlist trust-boundary). T-001 is documentation-only with no control-flow; acceptance-tests.md Notes classify TEST-001..004/015 as 'document conformance' reviewed by inspection, consistent with design.md Test Strategy item 1's own stated rationale, and is outside this check's 'functional task' scope.

acceptance-tests.md's Test Type column uses a finer-grained taxonomy (document conformance / configuration conformance / integration (fixture-driven) / hygiene-non-mutation / existing-suite regression / construction proof / review-time conformance / protected-file staging conformance / recorded manual verification) but every entry's declared technique matches its described scope, and all 21 TEST-IDs are anchored to a specific AC-NNN. Fixture-driven entries invoking the real check-model-freshness.sh against multiple components (script + stubbed gh wrapper + fixture files) are correctly labeled 'integration' (TEST-006/007/020/021); single-artifact checks (TEST-005 permissions text-marker, TEST-010 grep self-check, TEST-013 hash comparison) are correctly scoped rather than mislabeled integration/e2e; TEST-008 is correctly labeled a one-time recorded manual verification, not a CI-repeated automated test. No mismatch found.

T-003 (Risk: high) carries an explicit Rollback field (tasks.md ~510-514): 'reviewed revert of this task's commits PLUS a second human-copy application reverting .github/workflows/test.yml's registration line ... the same human-in-the-loop mechanism that added it, never a direct agent revert of the live protected file,' cross-referenced to infra-spec.md Rollback. This satisfies the rollback-plan requirement for the spec's only high/critical task.

CHANGELOG.md appears in all three tasks' Planned Files with no pairwise Blockers between T-001 and T-002; however design.md Global Constraints explicitly addresses this: each task creates its OWN independent ## Unreleased entry citing a different issue (#156/#158/#157) -- 'no create-then-append serialization across tasks... is needed (unlike epic-159-pillar-b's single shared-issue two-task entry)' -- and tasks.md Global Constraints mandates 'implement one task at a time,' removing the simultaneous-edit collision risk. docs/agent-capability-matrix.md is primarily owned by T-001 and conditionally touched by T-002's Commit B only 'if T-001 has landed and a genuine reference exists' (tasks.md ~382-384) -- a self-conditional, ordering-safe edit embedded in T-002's own scope text, not an uncoordinated collision. No two tasks claim the same primary file for the same purpose without either a Blockers relationship or an explicit, reasoned coordination mechanism.

T-003's Blockers: T-001 is a genuine content dependency: design.md Global Constraints and requirements.md Main Workflows state T-003's filed-issue body must cite the SAME canonical-source list T-001 documents, and design.md External Integrations notes the actual reference URLs are 'recorded in the task's implementation report' at task-implementation time (not fully pinned by requirements.md, which pins only the source NAMES) -- so a real risk of two divergent source-list copies exists absent the ordering. T-002's dependency on Pillar C's C1 (#149) landing on main is correctly recorded as an External Blocker in prose within the Blockers section (tasks.md ~430-433), matching requirements.md Field Definitions' explicit external-Blocker-vs-in-spec-Depends-On distinction, not a spurious or missing entry. T-002's conditional edit to docs/agent-capability-matrix.md (only if T-001 has landed) is self-guarded in its own scope text and does not require a formal Blockers entry.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. All three tasks (T-001 docs addition, T-002 data population, T-003 new automation feature) are net-new feature work per their Goal/Scope/Done-When text, not a bugfix, regression fix, debugging, failure-diagnosis, flaky-test-resolution, or incident-remediation task.
